text: Last class we wrote professional emails, a request and a follow-up.
Today we talk about numbers, prices, and contracts.

Hi Walyson, thank you for the quote. Honestly, your rate is a little higher than I expected for this volume.
I understand. My margin is small this season, but for a bigger volume I can offer a small discount on the figure.
That helps. If I increase my bid to two containers, what rate can you give me in dollars?
For two containers I can lower the price by four percent. That makes it cheaper than my first quote and more competitive than the market.
Good. Please put the new figure on the invoice, and keep the currency in dollars.
Of course. I will send the invoice today, and I think this rate is better than any other offer this week.
I will send you a quote and the invoice today.
Our price is more competitive than the market.
For a large volume, I can offer a small discount.
Prices increased by ten percent this month.
We trade in dollars, the usual currency.

Hi, this is Walyson. I am recording a quick note about a price quote I am preparing for a buyer in Europe. They asked for a quote for one container, so the volume is large. Our cost is high this season, so the margin is small. I will offer a rate of four thousand two hundred dollars per ton. That figure is a little lower than last month, because the market dropped by about five percent. If they order a bigger volume, I can give them a small discount, maybe three percent. I will send the quote and the invoice today, in dollars, the usual currency for coffee. I think our price is more competitive than our main rival, and it is cheaper than the offer they had last week. I really hope they accept the bid.

Hello, this is Marina with a short market update. Prices moved a lot this week. On Monday, the rate for premium coffee was three thousand nine hundred dollars per ton. By Friday, it had increased by eight percent, so it is now higher than last month. Cheaper grades also went up, but more slowly, only about two percent. Demand is strong, so the volume of orders is bigger than usual. My estimate is that prices will stay high for the next quarter. If you want a good margin, send your quote soon, because a discount today is safer than waiting. Remember to check the currency on every invoice, because the dollar is stronger than the euro right now. That is all for today.

First, check the volume the buyer wants and your margin. Next, calculate the rate and prepare a clear quote in the right currency. Then, if the volume is big, offer a small discount on the figure. After that, compare your price with the market to show it is more competitive. Finally, send the invoice and confirm the final bid.
